title: Hyperdimensional Computing for Energy-Efficient AI in Manufacturing
description: Investigated hyperdimensional computing (HDC) as an energy-efficient AI paradigm for smart manufacturing, building domain-aware tuning strategies for sustainable machining quality prediction.
features: Analyzed sensitivity of HDC parameters across manufacturing datasets
Proposed domain-aware tuning strategies
Demonstrated comparable accuracy to conventional ML models with orders-of-magnitude reductions in training time and energy consumption
Developed hyperdimensional computing models for manufacturing process quality prediction

title: Large Language Models for Extrapolative Modeling of Manufacturing Processes
description: Developed an LLM-based framework that combines literature-derived knowledge with limited experimental data to build extrapolative manufacturing process models.
features: Extracted process knowledge from literature using LLMs
Integrated knowledge with small experimental datasets
Enabled highly extrapolative manufacturing process modeling
Outperformed traditional machine learning approaches under limited data conditions

title: Enhanced Magnet-aided Laser Induced Plasma Micromachining (E-MLIP)
description: Studied magnetic-field-assisted laser induced plasma micromachining to improve feature resolution, machining depth, and material removal rate.
features: Introduced magnetic-field-assisted plasma micromachining
Developed physics-based mechanism model for material removal
Improved feature resolution and machining depth simultaneously
Implemented acoustics-based in-situ feature prediction

title: Operational Resilience in Additive Manufacturing under Cyberphysical Attacks
description: Developed AI based digital twin frameworks to detect and recover from cyberphysical attacks on additive manufacturing processes in real time.
features: Developed AI-based framework for real-time single-shot defect recovery in additive manufacturing
Designed geometric and process digital twins to maintain production quality during cyberattacks
Demonstrated resilience against stealthy cyberphysical attacks in manufacturing systems

title: Throughput scaling and thermomechanical behaviour in Multiplexed Fused Filament Fabrication (MF3)
description: Contributed to the development of a novel thermal model and toolpath synthesis for MF3, improving throughput and mechanical properties
features: Enhanced the understanding of throughput scaling in MF3
Simulated mechanical behavior using COMSOL
Created visualizations for the results

title: PSPOnto: An application ontology to aid in data-driven materials design
description: Proposed an ontology to standardize material informatics (MI) datasets.
features: Developed PSPOnto, an ontology for Process-Structure-Property (PSP) relations
Created knowledge base for MI datasets
Assembled corpus on manufacturing process parameters
Enabled efficient data storage and retrieval for PSP datasets

title: Case study of a connecting rod: evaluating different design approaches
description: Optimized the design of a connecting rod for stiffness and mass.
features: Compared literature-based, TPMS-based, topologically optimal, and generative design
TPMS-based IWP hollow structure found optimal
Performed peoperty homogenization for TPMS structures
Performed Design for Additive Manufacturing for the proposed geometries

title: Establishing inverse process-structure linkage using a deep convolutional neural networks (CNN) approach
description: Focused on validating the importance of microstructre metadata and demonstrated multi-input multi-output CNNs to inculcate that information.
features: Modeled process-structure relations for Ultrahigh carbon steel and Ni-Co alloy
Achieved best results using microstructure+metadata input
Identified and addressed challenges with process-structure-property relations dataset

title: Experimental thermal analysis of phase change materials (PCM) in domestic refrigerators
description: Investigated the application of PCM to improve the energy efficiency of domestic refrigerators by elongating its refrigeration cycles.
features: Designed and built experimental setup
Improved power consumption by 36.28%
Extended refrigeration cycle time by 104.34%
1st rank in College Project Expo 2021
